title: aira cespitosa var. ambigua
intro: Aira cespitosa var. ambigua, also known as sea oat grass, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the grass family Poaceae. It is native to Europe and Western Asia, and has been used for its medicinal properties for centuries. The plant is valued for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as soothe skin irritations and wounds. Aira cespitosa var. ambigua can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health issues, from digestive problems to fever relief.
